title: Assistant Housing Support Worker
description:
ASSISTANT SUPPORT WORKER - FEMALE SERVICE (ONLY FEMALE WORKERS)
Ashberry recruitment are currently hiring Female Housing support workers in the South Yorkshire area, to work for a well established organisation that prevents homelessness.
Your role:
- Assist with the delivery of a high-quality Programme of support which promotes client choice and Control.
- To address Client support needs, enable them to achieve positive life changes and ultimately empower them to lead more sustainable lifestyles
- To work well as part of a team
- May work on a 1:1 basis with clients such as taking them out for coffee, taking them to appointments etc.
- Engaging with The residents and creating a trusted relationship
- Basic cleaning may be required
- May be required to wash and change bedding in rooms
- Liase with manager and Senior support worker (s)
- CCTV monitoring required
- Undertake client welfare checks as directed
- Provide front of house support and reception duties, as well as administrative support including day to day office duties; including recording income to the service on the system.
- Assist the support team with sourcing and facilitating planned move on options for clients where appropriate.
- To maintain effective links with local authorities and all other relevant service providers.
- To ensure the Health and Safety of the environment and all clients and staff that use the service and any incidents are accurately and clearly reported in line with procedures.
The ideal Candidate:
- Must have an enhanced DBS
- Must have a years support worker experience in similar settings or transferrable evidence from previous work history if not support worker
- Full UK valid driving license, and access to a vehicle
- Experience in similar homelessness support work, domestic violence support, rough sleepers support, ex offenders support, mental health support, substance misuse support, benefits support, tenancy support, social services support and social housing support etc.
- The ability to communicate with service users, colleagues and visitors.
- The ability to maintain clear and accurate records of work undertaken and produce reports as required.
- Ability to assist in the provision of a secure, efficient and caring service and to act in a support capacity to project workers and managers.
Pay and shifts:
- 4 shift rota, 7am to 7pm / 9am to 9pm. with every other weekend and 2 sleep in shifts required in the month
- Temp to perm opportunity.
- £14.74-£17.52 per hour.
This job is open to female workers only due to the nature of the service and service users.
This role is to start as soon as possible and has the potential to become a permanent contract.
Please contact us if interested.
summary:
Female housing support role in Sheffield focused on homelessness prevention, client support and day-to-day service delivery.
Includes rota shifts, admin/front-of-house duties and a temp-to-perm pathway.

title: Relief Support Worker
description:
We are looking for fun, energetic and adaptable individuals to join our team providing support to people
with learning disabilities and/or autism at our supported living service
in the following areas of Doncaster
Conisbrough
Sprotbrough
Scawsby
Bently
Arksey
Askern
Skellow
Woodlands
Rossington
Bessacarr
Bawtry
You will work to support clients with daily tasks to promote independence, whilst enabling individuals to enjoy fulfilling and valued lives.
As a Support Worker you will provide outcome focused support to enable clients to lead fulfilling and valued lives, maintain their tenancies, participate in their local community, develop independent living skills and achieve their personal goals and desired outcomes.
You will help our clients to maximise their sense of wellbeing and independence through tailored, ongoing care and support and will help facilitate engagements with their peers and out in their local community utilising opportunities for personal development.
This is a perfect career opportunity for people who are keen to learn new skills, are seeking a rewarding job that will allow them to progress in the health and social care sector and develop their career.
The service provides support to people with a learning disability in their own home, as a Relief Support Worker you will assist with daily activities such as cooking and cleaning and with various activities, also support with appointments and shopping.
Vacancy Reference Number: 82338
Applications for this role must be submitted via the Creative Support website using the above vacancy reference number
Full training is provided, as is the opportunity to work towards QCF Diploma in Health and Social Care.
Our support workers are expected to work flexibly to meet the needs of the service, including weekends and Bank Holidays.
At least twelve months experience is essential for our relief roles.
Benefits of working with Creative Support:
A one-off bonus of £100 upon successful completion of the 4-month probationary period
20 days Annual Leave plus 8 days statutory bank holiday
Pension with company contribution
Company paid enhanced DBS for all staff
All our staff are supported 24/7 by our out-of-hours teams
Support to complete the nationally recognised Care Certificate and Social Care Diploma
summary:
Relief role in Doncaster supporting adults with learning disabilities and autism across supported living settings.
Covers daily living support, community access and tenancy-focused independence work.
